segments.text: My son, attend unto my wisdom, and bow thine ear to my understanding:
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: That thou mayest regard discretion, and that thy lips may keep knowledge.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: For the lips of a strange woman drop as an honeycomb, and her mouth is smoother than oil:
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: But her end is bitter as wormwood, sharp as a twoedged sword.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Her feet go down to death; her steps take hold on hell.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Lest thou shouldest ponder the path of life, her ways are moveable, that thou canst not know them.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Hear me now therefore, O ye children, and depart not from the words of my mouth.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Remove thy way far from her, and come not nigh the door of her house:
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Lest thou give thine honour unto others, and thy years unto the cruel:
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Lest strangers be filled with thy wealth; and thy labours be in the house of a stranger;
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: And thou mourn at the last, when thy flesh and thy body are consumed,
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: And say, How have I hated instruction, and my heart despised reproof;
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: And have not obeyed the voice of my teachers, nor inclined mine ear to them that instructed me!
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: I was almost in all evil in the midst of the congregation and assembly.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Drink waters out of thine own cistern, and running waters out of thine own well.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Let thy fountains be dispersed abroad, and rivers of waters in the streets.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Let them be only thine own, and not strangers' with thee.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Let thy fountain be blessed: and rejoice with the wife of thy youth.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Let her be as the loving hind and pleasant roe; let her breasts satisfy thee at all times; and be thou ravished always with her love.
segments.speaker: Solomon

footnotes: be thou…: Heb. err thou always in her love

segments.text: And why wilt thou, my son, be ravished with a strange woman, and embrace the bosom of a stranger?
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: For the ways of man are before the eyes of the LORD, and he pondereth all his goings.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: His own iniquities shall take the wicked himself, and he shall be holden with the cords of his sins.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: He shall die without instruction; and in the greatness of his folly he shall go astray.
segments.speaker: Solomon
